title: The Generative Toolkit Recap
concept: We have covered GAN basics, training dynamics (minimax, BCE), stable architectures (DCGAN, WGAN), controllable generation (cGAN, Pix2Pix, CycleGAN), and latent space editing.

exercises.explain: Our GAN toolkit consists of generators creating fake data and discriminators trying to spot the fakes. Let's review everything we've learned!
exercises.prompt: What are the two main competing networks in any GAN architecture?

options.text: A single neural network working alone
A database and a query processor

cards.body: You've mastered the art of adversarial networks! From simple random noise to beautifully styled translations like Day-to-Night photos, you now hold the keys to the entire generative toolkit. Let's do a quick, high-level victory lap of these powerful concepts.
cards.title: The Generative Journey
What is the GAN Toolkit?
cards.body: The Generative Toolkit is a collection of architectures and techniques (like DCGAN, WGAN, cGAN, and CycleGAN) used to train neural networks to create realistic data from noise or transform existing images.

Think of the toolkit like a master artist's studio. You have basic sketch pads (standard GANs), steady hand supports for clean lines (DCGAN/WGAN), stencils for specific requests (cGAN), and translation mirrors to paint Day into Night (CycleGAN).
cards.title: The Artist's Studio

cards.body: Imagine you want to change only the hair color of an AI-generated face. Instead of starting from scratch, you locate the 'hair vector' in the latent space and slide it, watching the hair change in real-time while keeping the face identical.
cards.title: Latent Space Magic

cards.body: Myth: To get different styles or controlled outputs, you always need paired training photos (like matching before/after shots). Fact: CycleGAN proves we can learn styles using unpaired collections, like matching random photos of horses with random photos of zebras!
cards.title: The Paired Data Myth

cards.body: Let's compare standard GAN training with controllable conditional generation (cGAN).
cards.title: Random vs. Guided

left.text: Generates random images from random noise without user input.

right.text: Generates specific images guided by a class label or user prompt.

options.text: By moving along specific direction vectors in the latent space
By completely retraining the generator from scratch
By deleting random layers in the discriminator

exercises.prompt: How do we edit specific features of a generated image without changing its overall identity?
exercises.explain: Latent space editing allows us to modify specific features (like adding glasses or changing expression) by moving along mathematical directions (vectors) in the latent noise space.

exercises.prompt: CycleGAN allows us to translate images from domain A to domain B even if we do not have paired training examples.
exercises.explain: CycleGAN is specifically designed to translate styles between two unpaired sets of images (like photos and paintings).

exercises.sentence: Wasserstein GAN (WGAN) improves stability by replacing the binary classification task of the standard [blank] with a critic scoring system.
exercises.explain: WGAN replaces the standard binary classifier with a critic that evaluates image quality using Earth Mover's Distance, avoiding training collapse.
exercises.prompt: Wasserstein GAN (WGAN) improves stability by replacing the binary classification task of the standard ___________ with a critic scoring system.

options.text: Converting a hand-drawn building outline into a realistic photo of a building using paired training data
Classifying whether an email is spam or not spam based on keywords

exercises.explain: Pix2Pix requires paired dataset examples (like a sketch directly matching a finished photo) to learn pixel-to-pixel translations.
exercises.prompt: Which of the following is a classic example of a Pix2Pix image-to-image translation task?

options.text: Controllable generation using cGAN and latent space vector math
Using basic GANs with no conditional labels or latent space controls

exercises.explain: By mastering the GAN toolkit, we can build custom generative solutions. Next, we will explore advanced evaluation metrics like FID and IS to scientifically measure how good our generated images really are!
exercises.prompt: If a designer wants to generate a shoe of a specific color and then dynamically add laces, which toolkit features should they use?
